expectations.text: Output contains all four sections: Mission, Goals, Premises, and Constraints as distinct labeled sections
expectations.evidence: Output has '### Mission', '### Goals', '### Premises', and '### Constraints' as distinct labeled sections within Phase 1. All four are clearly separated.

expectations.text: Mission or Premises captures that Godot is free/open-source and community-driven (not just 'a game engine')
expectations.evidence: Mission: 'Enable anyone to create games without barriers.' W-functor chain includes 'remove cost and access barriers'. Goal 3: 'Remain completely free and open source under the MIT license -- no royalties, no contracts, no vendor lock-in'. Goal 5: 'Maintain community-driven governance'. P2: 'A permissive (MIT) license is more valuable than a copyleft or source-available model'. P3: 'Community volunteers and donation-funded developers can sustain engine development'.

expectations.text: Goals section mentions cross-platform export (e.g. desktop, mobile, web) as a key capability goal
expectations.evidence: Goal 2: 'Support cross-platform export from a single project -- games export with one click to desktop (Linux, macOS, Windows), mobile (Android, iOS), web, and XR platforms; console export available via third-party partners.'

expectations.text: Constraints section includes the MIT license or equivalent open-source licensing as a hard constraint
expectations.evidence: Hard constraint C1: 'MIT license for all engine code -- no copyleft, no proprietary dependencies in core' (Source: 'Stated (README, LICENSE.txt, design philosophy)'). C2: 'All bundled third-party libraries must be MIT-compatible'.

expectations.text: The spec reflects game-engine-specific concerns (scripting language, editor, 2D/3D, physics, audio) — not generic software requirements
expectations.evidence: Game-engine-specific throughout: GDScript as purpose-built language (P4), node-based scene composition vs ECS (P5), separate 2D and 3D rendering pipelines (P8), 'built-in scripting, animation, tilemap, shader, debugger, and profiler editors' (Goal 4), 'editor runs on the game engine' (S8), Vulkan/D3D12/Metal/OpenGL rendering (L5), GDExtension API (Goal 6).

expectations.text: At least one non-obvious requirement is inferred (e.g. GDScript design goals, no royalties constraint, community governance implications)
expectations.evidence: P3: 'Community volunteers and donation-funded developers can sustain engine development at competitive quality' (inferred from foundation funding model). P5: 'Node-based inheritance is a viable alternative to entity-component-system (ECS) architecture' (inferred from design philosophy contrast). P6: 'Console manufacturers will continue allowing third-party porting' (inferred from console support page). P7: 'The SCons build system can scale to the project's growing codebase' (inferred from SConstruct 52KB build file).

expectations.text: Output shows evidence of bottom-up Chain of Knowledge expansion with multiple levels visible (e.g. L5 Topics through L1 Domains, or equivalent layered knowledge build-up from raw facts to domain-level synthesis)
expectations.evidence: Phase 0 contains full CoK expansion: L5 (Topics) with 22 items from README/design philosophy/features page/contributing/license/foundation/version.py/platform dir/modules dir, L4 (Areas) with patterns, L3 (Fields) with implementation mechanisms, L2 (Disciplines) with mandates, L1 (Domains) with disjointness verification. Saturation summary consolidates all levels.

expectations.text: Every constraint (both hard and soft) cites its source — who or what imposed it (e.g. 'MIT license file', 'README', 'governance docs', 'inferred from project history') — not just the constraint text alone
expectations.evidence: Every hard constraint has Source column: C1 'Stated (README, LICENSE.txt, design philosophy)', C4 'Stated (proposals evaluation criterion #4)', C6 'Inferred from .clang-format, .clang-tidy, .pre-commit-config.yaml in repo root', C7 'Stated (console page)', C8 'Inferred from SConstruct (52KB)'. Every soft constraint: S1 'Stated (CONTRIBUTING.md)', S6 'Inferred from platform directory and README', S8 'Stated (design philosophy)'.

claims.claim: Godot uses MIT license for all engine code
claims.evidence: Well-known fact about Godot; cited from README and LICENSE.txt

claims.claim: Console ports require third-party partners due to NDA conflicts with open-source model
claims.evidence: Cited from godotengine.org/consoles page; this is a well-documented Godot limitation

claims.claim: The Godot Foundation is a legally independent Dutch entity (Stichting Godot)
claims.evidence: Cited from godot.foundation; this is publicly documented

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted. The combination of domain-specificity checks, implicit requirement checks, and methodological checks (CoK, source citations) provides good discrimination.
